filled_template.title: Unable to commission new machines unless fabrics are pristine
environment: HA (2 combined region+rack controllers, 1 separate database server)
filled_template.description: Unless the fabrics from an earlier commissioning are cleaned up, the 50-maas-01-commissioning script fails during post processing with the error: maasserver.models.interface.MultipleObjectsReturned: get() returned more than one PhysicalInterface -- it returned 2!. This occurred in 3.7.1 when commissioning more than 2 servers, but in 3.7.2, it requires manual cleanup of fabrics between small batches of servers.
steps_to_reproduce: Commission a couple of servers that have multiple NICs (e.g., 4) so that it results in a set of fabrics being created.
Wait for this to complete and note that 50-maas-01-commissioning succeeds in a pristine environment.
Commission another couple of servers without cleaning up the fabrics from the previous step.
filled_template.expected_result: Commissioning should succeed for all servers regardless of existing fabrics.
filled_template.actual_result: Commissioning fails on the 50-maas-01-commissioning script during post-processing with a MultipleObjectsReturned error.
filled_template.notes: The reporter suggests the issue is due to the use of get_or_create() in the commissioning script hooks, specifically in maasserver/models/interface.py.

completeness_summary: The report is highly detailed, providing the exact MAAS version, environment (HA), a clear set of reproduction steps, and a full Python stack trace. It even references a related known issue regarding get_or_create() behavior.
clarifying_questions: Are the fabrics being created automatically by MAAS during discovery, or are they manually defined?
Do the NICs on these servers share MAC addresses or any other identifying characteristics that might cause a collision in the database?
Can you provide the output of 'maas $PROFILE fabrics read' after the first set of servers is commissioned?
